items.headline: Global Markets Plunge Amid Escalating Middle East Tensions and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: Equity markets worldwide experienced a significant downturn today, driven by a deepening risk-averse sentiment following military strikes in the Middle East. Crude oil prices surged, intensifying inflation fears and prompting investors to seek safe-haven assets. This geopolitical unrest overshadowed some positive economic indicators, leading to broad-based selling across major indices. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Surges on Strong Earnings and Buyback
items.summary: Dell Technologies jumped significantly after reporting strong Q4 results, providing upbeat FY27 guidance, and announcing a substantial US$30 billion share buyback program.

items.headline: Netflix Climbs Amid Deal Exit and Upgrades
items.summary: Netflix saw a notable increase in its stock price as it exited a deal with Warner, received a US$2.8 billion break fee, and garnered multiple analyst upgrades.

items.headline: Lockheed Martin Rallies with Defense Sector Gains
items.summary: Defense contractors, including Lockheed Martin, rallied as geopolitical tensions escalated, driving increased investor interest in the sector.

items.headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Plummets on Geopolitical Woes
items.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings experienced a sharp decline amidst concerns over higher oil prices impacting fuel costs and potential disruptions to travel due to Middle East conflict.

items.headline: CoreWeave Dips After Mixed Q4 and Analyst Cuts
items.summary: CoreWeave fell after reporting mixed Q4 results and facing several cautious analyst price target cuts, signaling investor concerns.

items.headline: Airlines Hit Hard by Soaring Crude Prices
items.summary: Major airlines like United Airlines and American Airlines saw their shares drop significantly as surging crude oil prices threatened to increase already substantial fuel bills.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices Higher and Risk-Off Sentiment
items.summary: The primary market driver today was the escalating conflict in the Middle East, with US-Israel strikes on Iran and subsequent retaliation. This has pushed Brent crude past $82 and WTI near $73, sparking fears of an oil shock and sustained inflation, prompting a rush to safe-haven assets like gold.

items.headline: Energy and Defense Sectors Lead Gains Amid Broader Market Sell-off
items.summary: In a defensive market, the energy sector, along with defense contractors, emerged as leaders, benefiting from the surge in oil prices and increased geopolitical uncertainty. Conversely, sectors sensitive to fuel costs and interest rates, such as airlines, cruise lines, and technology, experienced significant declines.

items.headline: US Manufacturing Shows Growth, Inflation Concerns Persist Ahead of Key Labor Data
items.summary: The US ISM Manufacturing Index for February indicated continued growth in the manufacturing sector, registering at 52.4%. However, higher-than-expected inflation data and rising oil prices are fueling concerns about the Federal Reserve's interest rate policy, with investors keenly awaiting the upcoming Non-Farm Payrolls report on Friday.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Q4 and Full-Year Results
items.summary: Several companies released earnings reports today. Sealed Air Corporation ($SEE) reported a 2.1% increase in Q4 net sales and 6.4% adjusted diluted EPS growth for the full year 2025. ADT announced a 1% rise in Q4 revenue and 19% adjusted EPS growth for the full year, along with a new $1.5 billion share repurchase plan. Senior ($SNR) also reported a 20% annual profit rise.
